requirements.user_story: As a NoPilot developer, I want the UI taste system to support multiple design sources through a pluggable adapter interface, so that the system is not locked to a single vendor.

acceptance_criteria.ears: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a DesignProvider interface with methods: generateScreen, generateVariants, extractDesignContext, createDesignSystem
THE SYSTEM SHALL ship with two implementations: StitchProvider and AgentHTMLProvider
WHILE Stitch MCP is available and configured, THE SYSTEM SHALL use StitchProvider as the default
WHEN a new DesignProvider implementation is registered, THE SYSTEM SHALL make it available for selection without modifying core flow logic

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Architectural foundation — all other features depend on this interface. Strategy pattern with runtime provider selection.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want to see at least 5 distinct visual style variants for each page, so that I can explore a wide range of aesthetic possibilities.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN UI taste exploration is triggered for a page, THE SYSTEM SHALL generate at least 5 style variants via the active DesignProvider
WHILE the project is greenfield (no existing frontend), THE SYSTEM SHALL use REIMAGINE creative range
WHILE the project has existing frontend, THE SYSTEM SHALL use REFINE creative range
WHEN using Stitch, THE SYSTEM SHALL use Gemini 3.1 Pro model for generation

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Primary Stitch API consumer. Each call to generate_variants with variantCount:5 is one API call.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want to preview design variants in an interactive browser interface with real dynamic effects, so that I can accurately evaluate each style.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN variants are ready, THE SYSTEM SHALL start a local HTTP server on an auto-detected available port
THE SYSTEM SHALL render variants via iframe embedding of actual HTML (not screenshots)
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide tab-based switching between all variants

THE SYSTEM SHALL provide device preview buttons for: iPhone SE (375x667), iPhone 16 Pro (393x852), iPhone 16 Pro Max (430x932), iPad mini (744x1133), iPad Pro 11 inch (834x1194), iPad Pro 13 inch (1024x1366), iPad Split View (507x1194), Slide Over (320x1194), Desktop (1440x900), Full screen
THE SYSTEM SHALL support side-by-side comparison of any 2 variants at the same device size
WHEN the user is in an SSH environment, THE SYSTEM SHALL display the URL; WHEN local, THE SYSTEM SHALL auto-open the browser

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Core UX component shared by Tier 1 and Tier 2. Node.js http module, auto port detection, HTML template generation.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want my design selection to automatically flow back to the CLI without manual copy-paste, so that the workflow is seamless.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user clicks select in the preview browser, THE SYSTEM SHALL POST the selection to a localhost endpoint
WHEN the CLI receives the POST, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the pending Promise and continue the discover flow
WHEN 30 minutes pass with no selection, THE SYSTEM SHALL timeout, close the server, and prompt the user in CLI

downstream_impact.tech_implications: HTTP POST endpoint on preview server. Promise-based async wait in CLI. Timeout cleanup.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want to refine designs iteratively through text feedback, hybrid style mixing, and history rollback, so that I can converge on exactly the style I want.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user submits text feedback, THE SYSTEM SHALL use it as prompt constraint for the next generation round

WHEN the user selects 3 variants for hybrid DNA analysis, THE SYSTEM SHALL call extractDesignContext on each, allow user to annotate preferred dimensions per variant, and synthesize a composite constraint
THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve all previous iteration rounds in memory during the session
WHEN the user requests rollback, THE SYSTEM SHALL restore that round's variants in the preview

downstream_impact.tech_implications: LLM DNA synthesis quality is the key risk. In-memory iteration history management. ACCEPT_COST: may need multiple tuning iterations.

requirements.user_story: As a developer adding features to an existing app, I want the system to detect and respect my current design language, so that new pages are visually consistent with existing ones.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the project contains CSS/SCSS/Tailwind config/theme files, THE SYSTEM SHALL analyze them and extract a style profile
WHEN the user provides screenshots of existing UI, THE SYSTEM SHALL use them as style reference for generation
WHEN existing style is detected, THE SYSTEM SHALL synthesize a designMd constraint document and apply it to all subsequent generation calls
WHILE no existing frontend is detected and no screenshots are provided, THE SYSTEM SHALL skip Phase 0 and proceed to full exploration

downstream_impact.tech_implications: CSS/Tailwind/SCSS parser needed. ACCEPT_COST: cover CSS + Tailwind + SCSS first, extend incrementally.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want all pages in my app to share a consistent design language after I select a style for the first page.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user selects a variant for the first page, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract Design DNA and create a design system via the DesignProvider
WHILE generating variants for subsequent pages, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply the established design system as a constraint
THE SYSTEM SHALL allow the user to override the inherited style for a specific page if explicitly requested

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Depends on Stitch design system API (create_design_system, apply_design_system). For Tier 2, designMd constraint propagation.

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want the selected design exported as standardized design tokens, so that the build phase can directly consume them.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN all pages are selected, THE SYSTEM SHALL export design tokens in W3C DTCG .tokens.json format to specs/mockups/tokens.json
THE SYSTEM SHALL include color palette, typography scale, spacing, border-radius, and shadow tokens
WHEN using Tier 2 (AgentHTML), THE SYSTEM SHALL export tokens as CSS custom properties format (simplified)

downstream_impact.tech_implications: designMd to DTCG conversion via LLM. Style Dictionary compatible output. Build phase reads tokens.json directly.

requirements.user_story: As a developer, I want downloaded mockup HTML files to be self-contained and use the correct fonts, so that they render correctly offline and long-term.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN HTML is downloaded from Stitch, THE SYSTEM SHALL replace font-family declarations with the user-specified font stack
WHEN HTML contains externally hosted images, THE SYSTEM SHALL download and convert them to base64 data URIs
WHEN HTML lacks responsive CSS (no media queries or container queries), THE SYSTEM SHALL inject basic responsive breakpoints

downstream_impact.tech_implications: HTML string manipulation, image download + base64 encoding, CSS injection. Edge cases with various HTML structures.

requirements.user_story: As a developer without Stitch configured, I want the UI taste system to still provide a useful experience, so that the feature works regardless of external dependencies.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN Stitch MCP is available, THE SYSTEM SHALL use Tier 1 (StitchProvider)
WHEN Stitch MCP is unavailable but browser is accessible, THE SYSTEM SHALL fall back to Tier 2 (AgentHTMLProvider) within 5 seconds
WHEN neither Stitch nor browser is available, THE SYSTEM SHALL fall back to Tier 3 (text-based taste questions)
WHEN Stitch API quota is exhausted mid-session, THE SYSTEM SHALL notify the user and switch to Tier 2 for remaining pages

THE Tier 2 AgentHTMLProvider SHALL produce mockups with designMd constraints to achieve quality defined as: correct base layout, coordinated color scheme, basic animation effects, passing human review checklist

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Provider availability detection. Automatic fallback chain. Tier 2 quality is high-risk. ACCEPT_COST: quality defined as layout correct + colors coordinated + basic animation, verified by human checklist.

requirements.user_story: As a product owner, I want to preview and select both dark and light mode variants, so that my app has a consistent dual-mode experience.

acceptance_criteria.ears: WHEN the user selects a variant, THE SYSTEM SHALL auto-generate the corresponding dark/light counterpart
THE preview engine SHALL provide a dark/light toggle button to switch between modes
WHEN the user is not satisfied with the auto-generated counterpart, THE SYSTEM SHALL allow manual adjustment or re-generation

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Stitch colorMode parameter toggle. Preview UI dark/light switch. Paired mockup storage.

requirements.user_story: As a new NoPilot user, I want clear documentation on how to configure Stitch and use the UI taste feature, so that I can get started quickly.

acceptance_criteria.ears: THE README SHALL include a section on Stitch MCP configuration (API key setup, .mcp.json placement)
THE README SHALL include a usage tutorial for the UI taste exploration flow with example screenshots
THE README SHALL document the three-tier degradation behavior

downstream_impact.tech_implications: Documentation only. Requires screenshots from working implementation.

invariants.statement: THE SYSTEM SHALL never write Stitch API keys to any file inside the project repository
THE SYSTEM SHALL never leave temporary preview files in the project directory after a discover session completes or is abandoned
All design artifacts saved to specs/mockups/ SHALL be self-contained with no external URL dependencies
THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain backward compatibility with discover.json v4.0 — projects without UI taste data SHALL continue to work without modification
THE SYSTEM SHALL never block the discover flow if a design provider fails — degradation SHALL be automatic
Each concurrent discover session SHALL use a unique HTTP server port
